varieties.name: 🧬 HYBRID = First-generation cross (F1), typically all-male or male-dominant for higher yields
Mary Washington 🏆
varieties.origin: USA, 1919. Developed by Dr. J.B. Norton, USDA, from the Washington strain for rust resistance.
varieties.traits: Medium to large spears, deep green color with pale purple tips and bracts, 15-20cm long, 1-2cm diameter.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good. Mix of male/female plants (approximately 50/50), female plants reduce overall productivity.
varieties.availability: Widely available in Australia - Bunnings, nurseries throughout Queensland, online seed suppliers (Eden Brothers, The Seed Collection Australia).

varieties.name: Conover's Colossal (Connovar's Colossal) 🏆
varieties.origin: USA, 1868. Developed by S.B. Conover, New York produce merchant. One of the oldest heirloom varieties still in cultivation.
varieties.traits: Very thick spears (up to 3.8cm diameter), stumpy stems, dark green tinged with purple, pointed bud tips.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields for a non-hybrid variety. Heavy cropper with graceful feathery foliage.
varieties.availability: Available as seeds in Australia - Baker Creek Heirloom Seeds, Premier Seeds Direct, online heirloom seed suppliers.

varieties.name: UC-157 (UC 157 F2) 🌱
varieties.origin: USA, 1978. University of California, Riverside. Developed for warm climate production.
varieties.traits: Pale green spears, medium-large diameter, smooth texture, tight tips, uniform stalks, 15-23cm long.
varieties.yield_notes: Exceptionally high. Male and female plants (standard hybrid), but highly productive.
varieties.availability: Available in Australia - Mountain Valley Growers (pots), True Leaf Market (seeds), select Australian seed suppliers.

varieties.name: UC-72 (Mary's Granddaughter)
varieties.origin: USA, UC Davis/Berkeley. Mary Washington improvement for heat tolerance.
varieties.traits: Dark green spears, fairly compact heads, slightly larger than Mary Washington, 15-30cm long, 1.25cm diameter.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy yields after establishment. Open-pollinated with male/female mix, but very productive.
varieties.availability: Available in Australia - True Leaf Market, Eden Brothers, David's Garden Seeds, St. Clare Heirloom Seeds, Premier Seeds Direct.

varieties.name: Atlas F1
varieties.origin: USA, 1980s. Walker Brothers, New Jersey. California x Eastern US male hybrid breeding program.
varieties.traits: Dark green smooth spears with slight purple at tips and butt, medium to large diameter, cylindrical shape, 18-23cm, tightly headed.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high. All-male hybrid, 20-50% higher yielding than UC-157. Vigorous early variety.

varieties.name: Apollo F1
varieties.origin: USA, 1984. California female x Eastern US male breeding program.
varieties.traits: Dark green spears with tight purple tips, ends and bracts, medium to large diameter, cylindrical shape with slight taper, smooth appearance.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high. All-male hybrid, early maturing, 25-50% yield increase over UC-157.
varieties.availability: Available in Australia - Urban Farmer, specialty asparagus suppliers, international nurseries.

varieties.name: Grande Hybrid F1
varieties.origin: USA, California breeding program for warm climate production.
varieties.traits: Green spears, medium-large diameter, uniform appearance.
varieties.yield_notes: High yields with good consistency throughout season.
varieties.availability: Limited in Australia - Gurney's (US), international suppliers, may require import or specialty order.

varieties.name: Jersey Giant F1
varieties.origin: USA, 1980s. Rutgers University, New Jersey breeding program. NOTE: Breeding discontinued, limited remaining stock.
varieties.traits: Thick meaty green spears with purple bracts, 18-23cm long, very uniform.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high. All-male hybrid (mostly male), produces up to 2x yield of standard varieties.

varieties.name: Jersey Knight F1
varieties.origin: USA, 1980s. Rutgers University, New Jersey. NOTE: Breeding discontinued, limited remaining stock.
varieties.traits: Green spears with purple bracts, large diameter, best spear quality of Jersey hybrids, 18-25cm long.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high. Mostly male plants, produces 3-4x more than standard varieties.

varieties.name: Jersey Supreme F1
varieties.origin: USA, Rutgers University, New Jersey. Latest of Jersey series. NOTE: Breeding discontinued.
varieties.traits: Green spears, early maturing, uniform medium to large diameter.
varieties.yield_notes: High. All-male variety, produces spears earlier in season.
varieties.availability: VERY LIMITED - Variety discontinued. Final remaining stock at select retailers. Not recommended for new plantings.

varieties.name: Millennium (Guelph Millennium) F1
varieties.origin: Canada, developed by Prof. David Wolyn, University of Guelph, Ontario. Released early 2000s.
varieties.traits: Pure solid green spears with purple bracts (moderate anthocyanin), thick tender straight spears, cylindrical uniform size, 18-25cm.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high and consistent. All-male hybrid (100%), highly productive, equals Jersey varieties at maturity. Trials in Michigan showed 2094 lbs/acre vs Jersey Giant 1287 lbs/acre. Royal Horticultural Society Award of Garden Merit.
varieties.availability: Available in Australia - Stark Brothers, Burpee, Nourse Farms, Veseys, West Coast Seeds, international suppliers. Good availability.

varieties.name: Spartacus F1
varieties.origin: Modern hybrid bred to replace Jersey varieties.
varieties.traits: Green spears with purple tips and bracts, medium size, straight, long.
varieties.yield_notes: High yields. All-male hybrid.
varieties.availability: Available internationally, limited in Australia, may require import from specialty suppliers.

varieties.name: Purple Passion F1 🧬
varieties.origin: Bred from Italian heirloom 'Violet di Albenga' (natural genetic mutation), open-pollinated purple variety.
varieties.traits: Deep purple spears (color fades to green when cooked), attractive appearance, purple tips and bracts.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good. Male and female plants (60% male), fewer but larger spears. Less productive than all-male green varieties but compensates with superior flavor.

varieties.name: Sweet Purple
varieties.origin: Italian heritage, cold-hardy purple variety.
varieties.traits: Bold purple color throughout, distinctive appearance.
varieties.yield_notes: Good. Male and female plants mix.
varieties.availability: Available - True Leaf Market (crowns), international suppliers, select Australian nurseries.

varieties.name: Erasmus F1
varieties.origin: UK, 2012. First all-male purple hybrid asparagus, breakthrough variety.
varieties.traits: Deep purple spears with tight tips, mild color, brilliant purple when raw.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high - revolutionary for purple varieties. All-male hybrid, yields 2-3x other purple varieties, on par with green varieties.
varieties.availability: Limited in Australia - Gurney's (US), European suppliers, specialty import may be required.

varieties.name: Precoce D'Argenteuil (Early Argenteuil) 🏆
varieties.origin: France, 1885. Vilmorin-Andrieux selection from 'Giant Dutch Purple'. Argenteuil district near Paris, famous asparagus region since 1800s.
varieties.traits: Thick rose-purple spears when unblanched, attractive rosy pink tips, pale green when blanched (White Gold), 60-90cm tall at maturity.
varieties.yield_notes: Good to very good. Male and female mix, but reliably productive.

varieties.name: Palmetto 🏆
varieties.origin: USA, Southern states. Derived from Argenteuil selections. Pre-1900, long popular in the American South.
varieties.traits: Large variety, almost no culls (nearly all shoots uniform large size), green spears.
varieties.yield_notes: Good, with exceptional uniformity of spear size.

varieties.name: Barr's Mammoth (Barr's Philadelphia Mammoth) 🏆
varieties.origin: USA, Pennsylvania. Originated by Crawford Barr, prominent market gardener. Pre-1900.
varieties.traits: Grows to largest size, very early variety, light colored spears.
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, early bearing.

varieties.name: Martha Washington & Waltham Washington 🏆
varieties.origin: USA, early 1900s. Part of Washington strain developed for rust resistance. Same breeding program as Mary Washington.
varieties.traits: Long thick emerald green spears, Martha has extra long harvesting season.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Open-pollinated varieties with 50/50 male/female mix, lower yielding than all-male hybrids. Female plants reduce productivity.

varieties.name: Gijnlim F1
varieties.origin: Netherlands, Limgroup breeding. Earliest 100% male variety alongside Frühlim.
varieties.traits: Bright green thick straight spears, excellent spear quality, medium to thick diameter, well closed tips.
varieties.yield_notes: Unbeatable yield, very high productivity. All-male (100%). Royal Horticultural Society Award of Garden Merit.

varieties.name: Backlim F1
varieties.origin: Netherlands, Limgroup. Primarily for white asparagus production.
varieties.traits: Thick vibrant green spears when grown green, fine straight stems, firm tips, thick with well closed tips, mid to late season.
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, reliable yields. All-male (100%). Good half of production is 20-28mm class. Royal Horticultural Society Award of Garden Merit.

varieties.name: Mondeo F1
varieties.origin: Germany. All-male hybrid.
varieties.traits: Green spears, early season cropping.
varieties.yield_notes: Great yields. All-male (100%).

varieties.name: Thielim F1
varieties.origin: Netherlands, Limgroup. Recent introduction.
varieties.traits: White asparagus production specialist, average earliness.
varieties.yield_notes: Good production capacity. All-male (100%).
varieties.availability: European suppliers - Lindflora (Netherlands), Dutch nurseries. Import required.

varieties.name: Viking KB3
varieties.origin: Mary Washington-type hybrid, newer variety.
varieties.traits: Green spears with purple tips, sturdy variety, 20-25cm length.
varieties.yield_notes: High yields, many stalks. Mix of male/female plants. Popular commercial option.
varieties.availability: Limited availability, specialty suppliers, may require searching international sources.

varieties.name: Alternative: Asparagus racemosus (Shatavari)
varieties.yield_notes: 5-6 tonnes dry roots per acre (medicinal use)
varieties.availability: Widely available in Kerala - local nurseries, Kerala Agricultural University, Ayurvedic medicinal plant nurseries, horticulture departments

varieties.name: 5. Conover's Colossal (heirloom) - Historic Queensland variety, well-adapted
6. Palmetto (heirloom) - Southern heritage, warm climate proven
